evals.prompt: I'm running three Claude Code agents in parallel inside cmux and I want a bash script that creates a new workspace for each one, splits the pane into three columns, and then sends 'claude' followed by Enter to each surface. How do I do this?
evals.expected_output: A working bash script using cmux new-workspace, cmux new-split, cmux list-surfaces --json to get IDs, and cmux send / cmux send-key enter for each surface. Should include cmux identify --json safety check.

assertions.name: uses-cmux-send-with-surface-flag
assertions.description: The answer uses `cmux send --surface` to target specific panes rather than just `cmux send`

assertions.name: includes-safety-check
assertions.description: The answer includes cmux identify or cmux ping to verify context before acting

assertions.name: uses-new-split-for-columns
assertions.description: The answer uses `cmux new-split right` (or similar direction) to create columns

assertions.name: sends-enter-key
assertions.description: The answer uses cmux send-key enter (or send-key-surface) to submit the command

evals.prompt: I have a long-running npm build script and I want cmux to ping me (show a notification) when it finishes, whether it succeeds or fails. Can you write a shell wrapper function I can drop into my .zshrc?
evals.expected_output: A shell function that runs the command, captures the exit code, and calls `cmux notify` with an appropriate title and body for success vs failure. Should handle the case where cmux is not available.

assertions.name: uses-cmux-notify
assertions.description: The answer uses `cmux notify --title ... --body ...` for the notification

assertions.name: differentiates-success-failure
assertions.description: The answer sends different notifications for success vs failure (checks exit code)

assertions.name: graceful-when-cmux-absent
assertions.description: The answer checks if cmux is available before calling it (avoids hard error when not in cmux)

evals.prompt: How do I use cmux's sidebar to show a live progress bar and status badge while my CI pipeline script runs? I want to show 'building' in orange while compiling, then 'done' in green when finished.
evals.expected_output: Explanation and example commands using cmux set-progress and cmux set-status with --icon and --color flags, plus cmux clear-progress at the end. Should show both the status badge and progress bar workflows.

assertions.name: uses-set-status-with-color
assertions.description: The answer uses `cmux set-status` with a --color flag for the orange/green badges

assertions.name: uses-set-progress
assertions.description: The answer uses `cmux set-progress` with a numeric value and --label

assertions.name: clears-progress-at-end
assertions.description: The answer includes `cmux clear-progress` after the pipeline finishes
